fear: That his regiment will be the unit that proved the AEF cannot fight — that his name will appear in French dispatches as a cautionary example.
name: Colonel Hanson Ely
voice: Short declarative sentences. Profanity used as punctuation. Never asks a question he does not already know the answer to.
secrets: He received a written warning from G-2 intelligence three days ago suggesting a possible German intelligence asset in the forward signal detachment — he filed it without acting on it because he did not want the assault delayed
knowledge: He received a verbal report at 05:30 that a wire-layer had found an anomaly in the forward line — he dismissed it as pre-battle nerves
He knows Sergeant Voss by reputation as a competent but morally flexible NCO who has twice been reduced in rank for conduct unbecoming
He has the authority to arrest any officer or enlisted man in the forward area without referral to higher command
publicFace: A blocky, red-faced West Pointer who communicates primarily through controlled fury and monosyllables. His men fear him more than they fear the Germans, which is precisely the effect he cultivates.
trustLogic: Responds to demonstrated competence under pressure. A player who brings him two solid pieces of evidence and a clear chain of custody will get his full attention and authority. Bring him rumor and he will throw you out of his command post.
privateGoal: To take Cantigny, hold it against everything the Germans throw, and write his name into the history of the United States Army. He will accept casualties. He will not accept failure.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Stares. Does not speak until the questioner finishes, then asks one precise counter-question
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will never admit he suppressed the G-2 warning
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Stands up. Physically advances. Voice drops to a near-whisper that is more frightening than shouting
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Immovable. Will simply repeat his rank and demand to speak to Pershing personally
